title: failed to start container fedora-toolbox-34
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

can't use toolbox in Fedora 34 Workstation.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. enter a toolbox failed
```sh
$ toolbox enter
Error: failed to start container fedora-toolbox-34
```

2. start toolbox mannaully failed 
```sh
$ podman  --log-level debug start fedora-toolbox-34
INFO[0000] podman filtering at log level debug
DEBU[0000] Called start.PersistentPreRunE(podman --log-level debug start fedora-toolbox-34)
DEBU[0000] Reading configuration file "/usr/share/containers/containers.conf"

DefaultMountsFile: DNSServers:[] DNSOptions:[] DNSSearches:[] EnableKeyring:true EnableLabeling:true Env:[PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin TERM=xterm] EnvHost:false HTTPProxy:true Init:false InitPath: IPCNS:private LogDriver:k8s-file LogSizeMax:-1 NetNS:slirp4netns NoHosts:false PidsLimit:2048 PidNS:private SeccompProfile:/usr/share/containers/seccomp.json /usr/sbin/conmon /usr/local/bin/conmon /usr/local/sbin/conmon /run/current-system/sw/bin/conmon] DetachKeys:ctrl-p,ctrl-q EnablePortReservation:true Env:[] EventsLogFilePath:/run/user/1000/libpod/tmp/events/events.log EventsLogger:journald HooksDir:[/usr/share/containers/oci/hooks.d] ImageBuildFormat:oci ImageDefaultTransport:docker:// ImageParallelCopies:0 ImageDefaultFormat: InfraCommand: InfraImage:k8s.gcr.io/pause:3.5 InitPath:/usr/libexec/podman/catatonit LockType:shm MultiImageArchive:false Namespace: NetworkCmdPath: NetworkCmdOptions:[] NoPivotRoot:false NumLocks:2048 OCIRuntime:crun OCIRuntimes:map[crun:[/usr/bin/crun /usr/sbin/crun /usr/local/bin/crun /usr/local/sbin/crun /sbin/crun /bin/crun And the log of conmon
```
Apr 21 11:37:28 x240 conmon[3431617]: conmon 6c2dbe3e2563e0c7aadd <ndebug>: failed to write to /proc/self/oom_score_adj: Permission denied
Apr 21 11:37:28 x240 conmon[3431618]: conmon 6c2dbe3e2563e0c7aadd <ninfo>: addr{sun_family=AF_UNIX, sun_path=/proc/self/fd/13/attach}

Apr 21 11:37:28 x240 conmon[3431619]: conmon 6c2dbe3e2563e0c7aadd <nwarn>: Failed to chown stdin
Apr 21 11:37:28 x240 conmon[3431618]: conmon 6c2dbe3e2563e0c7aadd <error>: Failed to create container: exit status 1
```


3. try start with crun mannually?
```sh
$ podman unshare
# podman mount fedora-toolbox-34
/home/mike/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/c8952db1972fbc1f9fa97948fcf90c92dc2fa50c2512943229de80e327e0140c/merged
# /usr/bin/crun --debug --systemd-cgroup create --bundle /home/mike/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay-containers/6c2dbe3e2563e0c7aaddb008a455242c06ff66693cbb8b29c61f1bdef5056012/userdata 6c2dbe3e2563e0c7aaddb008a455242c
2021-04-21T03:43:43.000501185Z: make `/home/mike/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/c8952db1972fbc1f9fa97948fcf90c92dc2fa50c2512943229de80e327e0140c/merged` private: Permission denied
```

But use mount command is OK
```
# findmnt -o +PROPAGATION  /home/mike/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/c8952db1972fbc1f9fa97948fcf90c92dc2fa50c2512943229de80e327e0140c/merged
TARGET                                                                                                                     SOURCE         FSTYPE              OPTIONS                            PROPAGATION
/home/mike/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/c8952db1972fbc1f9fa97948fcf90c92dc2fa50c2512943229de80e327e0140c/merged fuse-overlayfs fuse.fuse-overlayfs rw,nodev,noatime,user_id=0,group_i private
#  mount --make-private /home/mike/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/c8952db1972fbc1f9fa97948fcf90c92dc2fa50c2512943229de80e327e0140c/merged
# echo $?
0
```

comments.body:
I'm not sure which part is broken, toolbox? podman? conmon? crun?

So I just ticket issue here.

Looks like c/storage - we're failing to make the eventual mountpoint for the container private.

@nalind Any ideas here?
I just tried this on my fedora 34 box and it works fine.

For running a container, the storage library doesn't change the propagation of the rootfs on its own.  Based on the "try start with crun" check, it looks like this error is coming from the runtime, which is trying to make its root-directory-to-be private before pivoting into it.  I'm not getting an error when I try it here here, either (give or take https://github.com/containers/toolbox/issues/664), but then my kernel version is slightly different (5.11.11-200.fc33 vs 5.11.13-300.fc34), and my home directory is on xfs.

Yes, I think this is not a general issue.  My home fs is on Btrfs, and I've checked use image of fedora-toolbox:33, and `podman system reset`, both have problem alike.

I found this only error with --userns=keep-id
```sh
$ podman  run --userns=keep-id redis
Error: make `/home/mike/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/5cbd5709646f4804c4e2fcc00d905ceca2cafe12899fa0a7831a4634ef2af9fd/merged` private: Permission denied: OCI permission denied
$ podman  run -d redis
a7a02da66491717da44bb68093ddf1e5cdcb1026c5c4fa37ad6ccd50cea6be91
```

Any chance you can try with `--user` and a non-root user instead (`--user 1000:1000` would do nicely)? `--userns=keep-id` does two things (alters UID mapping, and sets user in the container to a non-root user); I'm wondering which is causing this.
@mheon . It works with podman run -d --user 1000:1000 redis.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

> @pendulm Any further word on this?

No, I'm not looking at this recently. But I have a reproducible environment. I will dig this when I have some time.

I found the root case of This issue.
My container dir has a wrong group, and  make keep-id container start failed.
```sh
[mike@x240 ~]$ ls -ld /home/mike/.local/share/containers/                                                                               │[mike@x240 ~]$ ps
drwx------. 1 mike docker 0 Jul 15 20:29 /home/mike/.local/share/containers/                                                            │    PID TTY          TIME CMD
[mike@x240 ~]$ chown mike:mike /home/mike/.local/share/containers/                                                                      │  25996 pts/7    00:00:00 bash
[mike@x240 ~]$ ls -ld /home/mike/.local/share/containers/                                                                               │  27530 pts/7    00:00:00 ps
drwx------. 1 mike mike 0 Jul 15 20:29 /home/mike/.local/share/containers/
```

And In this case the logic break in here, I've no idea what is the reason.
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/282373/125789299-97051f6f-8730-44e8-8ab6-aadda2ab431f.png)
Can you provide a pointer to where that code lives?

> Can you provide a pointer to where that code lives?

https://github.com/containers/crun/blob/main/src/libcrun/linux.c#L1910
It breaks in crun code
After I fix the group  mannually, this issue got rid of. So I note this new find here in case someone  get same problem incaution.
I also encountered this issue. In my case the group on my home dir != the user; an error message for this would be very helpful.
Please consider opening a PR with this fix.
